paragraphs.cn: 所以，当总统谈论这个长篇演讲时，他会被所有这些小社区的烟花包围，从费尔法克斯到马里兰，再到各地。我不知道他怎么能完成演讲。我觉得他们没有考虑周全。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I mean I grew up in Boston, like, seeing the symphony and the fireworks on the esplanade (ph)
paragraphs.cn: 我在波士顿长大，比如在河滨大道看交响乐和烟花。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I care -- I like love July 4th, I can tell you.
paragraphs.cn: 我很在乎 --  -- 我热爱7月4日，我可以告诉你。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And as an immigrant, I love July 4th. I think that everybody feels like, why do the vibes have to be off on something that should have been politically neutral? And now the events, that Freedom 250 has made, and I'm saying Freedom 250 --
paragraphs.cn: 作为一名移民，我热爱7月4日。我觉得每个人都觉得，为什么本应政治中立的事情氛围会变得不对劲？而现在，"自由250"举办的活动 --  -- 我说的是"自由250" --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: I know, you're drawing your line there.
paragraphs.cn: 我知道，你在这里划清界限。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I draw a line by congressionally approved versus not. I like that line because it's like my tax dollars and who represents me, is like, it didn't have to be this way. You didn't have to have a moment where Martina McBride, of all people, has to be like, you know what, I'm out. This is too political.
paragraphs.cn: 我根据国会批准与否来划清界限。我喜欢这条界限，因为这关系到我的税款和代表我的人，事情本不必如此。你本不必经历这样一个时刻，连玛蒂娜·麦克布莱德这样的人都要说，你知道吗，我退出。这太政治化了。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: But to your larger point, I think once you get outside the beltway, where America is going to be celebrating this, in small towns across the country, I mean, if you want a call-out, leave Washington --
paragraphs.cn: 但回到你更大的观点，我认为一旦你走出华盛顿特区，美国各地都会庆祝这个节日，在全国的小城镇里，我的意思是，如果你想被点名，就离开华盛顿 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Don't go through DCA, though --
paragraphs.cn: 不过别走里根国家机场 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Because it will be shut down. But we --
paragraphs.cn: 因为那里会关闭。但我们 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, because airspace is shut down.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，因为空域会关闭。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: The airspace will be shut down. But leave Washington.
paragraphs.cn: 空域会关闭。但离开华盛顿吧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It doesn't feel the tiniest bit defeatist to you to just say, welp, states, it's your turn because we couldn't do it.
paragraphs.cn: 你难道不觉得说"好吧，各州，轮到你们了，因为我们做不到"有一点点失败主义吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: No, no, I -- laboratories of democracy.
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，我 --  -- 民主的实验室。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: But I think people are tired of Donald Trump's chaos and tired of all of this -- the antics anyways. And so I think that people have turned to their communities writ large the last year anyway. So, I think --
paragraphs.cn: 但我认为人们已经厌倦了唐纳德·特朗普的混乱，厌倦了这一切 --  -- 至少是那些滑稽行为。所以我认为过去一年里，人们已经转向了他们的社区。所以，我认为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Or to the Taylor Swift wedding.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。或者转向泰勒·斯威夫特的婚礼。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: And so I think people are paying attention to what's going on in their communities. They'll enjoy their own parades and their own fireworks. And that's what's so great about our country.
paragraphs.cn: 所以我认为人们正在关注他们社区里发生的事情。他们会享受自己的游行和烟花。这就是我们国家的伟大之处。

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Cornish
paragraphs.text: And maybe that is a good thing for celebration of colonial independence.
paragraphs.cn: 也许这对庆祝殖民独立来说是件好事。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: We're resilient. We're resilient.
paragraphs.cn: 我们很有韧性。我们很有韧性。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Shout out to the colonies.
paragraphs.cn: 向殖民地致敬。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Meghan's right, Donald Trump, community builder.
paragraphs.cn: 梅根说得对，唐纳德·特朗普，社区建设者。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: Oh, OK. Yes. That's --
paragraphs.cn: 哦，好吧。是的。那是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is that what's in your group chat? We're doing group chats next.
paragraphs.cn: 那是你群聊里的内容吗？我们接下来要聊群聊。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: So, in my group chat is McDonald's in honor of America's 250, has brought back the fried apple pie.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，在我的群聊里，麦当劳为了纪念美国250周年，重新推出了炸苹果派。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Oh, my God, you are the second person in the group chat to bring up this fried apple creation.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，天哪，你是群里第二个提到这种油炸苹果派的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: No, it's -- they got rid of it. They got rid of it in 1992. It's now back.
paragraphs.cn: 不，它 --  -- 他们之前停售了。1992年停售的。现在又回来了。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: And it is also so delicious.
paragraphs.cn: 而且它非常好吃。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: But, you know, I love fast food almost as much as I love Bravo. So, that is why I'm talking about McDonald's fried apple pie. It is so good. I had it last night.
paragraphs.cn: 但是，你知道，我爱快餐几乎和爱Bravo一样多。所以这就是为什么我在谈论麦当劳的油炸苹果派。它太好吃了。我昨晚吃了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Shout out. I hope, I hope you can get spawn con (ph) of that. I hope the next time you come here you just have like an arm full of breakfast for us.
paragraphs.cn: 大声喊出来。我希望，我希望你能弄到那个。我希望下次你来的时候，你能给我们带一堆早餐。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: I should have -- I should have brought you one. I'm sorry.
paragraphs.cn: 我应该 --  -- 我应该给你带一个的。抱歉。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: They are very good. And they burn. They burn. You got to wait. Wait.

paragraphs.text: So, I tried to think of a public policy thing to tie this next -- my group chat into.

paragraphs.text: Which is, there's a Norwegian study out that in Norway they have given eighth graders tablets. So, nobody's reading books anymore. And what they've found is that the retention level of the students is subpar to the rest of students in Europe because they're not reading books. And the reason I bring that up is because I was told that I had to watch "Off Campus."
